name: Mount Pleasant
h1: Personal Injury Lawyer Serving Mount Pleasant, SC
meta_title: Mount Pleasant Personal Injury Lawyer | Roden Law | Free Consult
meta_description: Injured in Mount Pleasant? Roden Law's Charleston personal injury lawyers serve Mount Pleasant, Shem Creek, and the East Cooper area. Free consultation. No fees unless we win.
roads: US-17 at Houston Northcutt Boulevard is one of the busiest intersections in the East Cooper area, generating frequent rear-end and turning collisions during peak hours. The IOP Connector bridge funnels heavy beach traffic from Mount Pleasant to Isle of Palms, creating dangerous merge points at both ends. The Ravenel Bridge approaches on the Mount Pleasant side see congestion-related accidents, especially during morning and evening commutes. The Highway 41 corridor through outer Mount Pleasant has become increasingly dangerous as rapid residential development has outpaced road infrastructure improvements.
hospitals:
East Cooper Medical Center — 2000 Hospital Dr, Mount Pleasant, SC 29464 — (843) 881-0100
Roper St. Francis Mount Pleasant Hospital — 3500 Highway 17 North, Mount Pleasant, SC 29466 — (843) 606-7000
landmarks: Ravenel Bridge, Shem Creek, Patriots Point Naval & Maritime Museum, Boone Hall Plantation, Mount Pleasant Town Centre, I'On neighborhood, Old Village
service_area: Serving Mount Pleasant, the Old Village, I'On, Park West, Rivertowne, Dunes West, Snee Farm, Belle Hall, Carolina Park, and surrounding East Cooper communities.
content_hook:
Mount Pleasant is the largest suburb in the Charleston metro with approximately 95,000 residents, and it continues to grow rapidly along the Highway 41 corridor. The community sits across the Arthur Ravenel Jr. Bridge from downtown Charleston, and that bridge — along with US-17 — channels tens of thousands of commuters and tourists through the area daily. This volume of traffic, combined with ongoing development and the convergence of beach-bound visitors heading to Isle of Palms and Sullivan's Island, makes Mount Pleasant one of the most active areas for motor vehicle accidents in Charleston County.

Roden Law's Charleston office at 127 King Street is just minutes across the Ravenel Bridge from Mount Pleasant. Our personal injury attorneys regularly handle cases involving accidents on US-17, the IOP Connector, and throughout the East Cooper area. If you've been injured in Mount Pleasant, we provide free consultations and work on a contingency fee basis — you pay nothing unless we recover compensation for you.

faqs.question: What should I do after a car accident on the Ravenel Bridge?
faqs.answer: If you can safely do so, move your vehicle to the emergency shoulder or pull-off area. Call 911 immediately — the bridge is patrolled by the South Carolina Highway Patrol. Do not attempt to walk on the bridge outside your vehicle. Seek medical attention even if your injuries seem minor, as the adrenaline from a bridge accident can mask symptoms. Contact Roden Law at (843) 790-8999 for a free case review.

faqs.question: Which hospital should I go to after an accident in Mount Pleasant?
faqs.answer: The two primary options are East Cooper Medical Center on Hospital Drive and Roper St. Francis Mount Pleasant Hospital on Highway 17 North. For severe trauma, MUSC Medical Center in downtown Charleston is the region's only Level I Trauma Center and is approximately 15 minutes from most of Mount Pleasant via the Ravenel Bridge.

faqs.question: How long do I have to file a personal injury claim after an accident in Mount Pleasant?
faqs.answer: South Carolina has a 3-year statute of limitations for personal injury claims (S.C. Code § 15-3-530). This means you must file your lawsuit within 3 years of the date of your accident. However, evidence deteriorates and witnesses become harder to locate over time, so contacting an attorney as early as possible strengthens your case.

faqs.question: Can Roden Law handle my Mount Pleasant accident case from your Charleston office?
faqs.answer: Yes. Our Charleston office at 127 King Street is just minutes from Mount Pleasant via the Ravenel Bridge. All Mount Pleasant personal injury cases are filed in Charleston County Circuit Court, which is the same court our attorneys practice in daily. We serve all of the East Cooper area from our Charleston location.

name: North Charleston
h1: Personal Injury Lawyer Serving North Charleston, SC
meta_title: North Charleston Personal Injury Lawyer | Roden Law | Free Consult
meta_description: Injured in North Charleston? Roden Law handles car, truck & pedestrian accident cases near I-26, Rivers Ave & Ashley Phosphate Rd. Free consultation.
court: North Charleston Municipal Court / Charleston County Circuit Court
roads: The intersection of Ashley Phosphate Road and I-26 is the single most dangerous intersection in the tri-county Charleston area, with 629 crashes recorded between 2011 and 2015 and continuing high collision rates. The intersection is large, with signal lights spaced far apart and faded lane markings that confuse drivers. I-26 Exit 203 at College Park Road is the second most dangerous intersection in the region, with over 380 accidents during the same period. Rivers Avenue is one of the most dangerous roads in South Carolina for pedestrians and cyclists, with multiple fatal pedestrian crashes in 2024 and 2025. Dorchester Road connects residential neighborhoods to commercial areas and sees frequent congestion-related crashes during commute hours.
hospitals:
Trident Medical Center (Level II Trauma Center) — 9330 Medical Plaza Dr, North Charleston, SC 29406 — (843) 797-7000
HCA Healthcare Centre Pointe ER — International Blvd & Emmett Davis Jr Ave, North Charleston
Roper St. Francis North Charleston — 7832 Rivers Avenue, North Charleston, SC 29406 — (843) 824-8733
landmarks: Charleston International Airport, North Charleston Coliseum & PAC, Tanger Outlets, Park Circle neighborhood, Joint Base Charleston, North Charleston City Hall
service_area: Serving North Charleston, Park Circle, Olde North Charleston, Dorchester-Waylyn, Liberty Hill, Ferndale, Oak Terrace, and surrounding communities along the I-26 and Rivers Avenue corridors.
content_hook:
North Charleston is the third-largest city in South Carolina with approximately 118,000 residents and some of the highest traffic volumes in the state. The convergence of I-26, I-526, Rivers Avenue, and Ashley Phosphate Road creates a complex network of high-speed interchanges and commercial corridors where serious accidents occur regularly. In the first half of 2024 alone, Charleston County recorded 24 roadway deaths.

The Ashley Phosphate Road and I-26 intersection has been identified as the most dangerous intersection in Berkeley, Charleston, and Dorchester counties combined. Rivers Avenue has earned a particularly grim reputation for pedestrian fatalities, with multiple fatal pedestrian crashes in recent years. Roden Law's personal injury attorneys handle cases throughout North Charleston and understand the specific intersection designs, traffic patterns, and road conditions that contribute to these accidents. Contact us at (843) 790-8999 for a free case review.

faqs.question: Why is the Ashley Phosphate Road and I-26 intersection so dangerous?
faqs.answer: This intersection handles over 167,000 vehicles daily. The intersection is unusually large, with signal lights spaced far apart — making it difficult for drivers to tell which signal applies to their lane. Faded road markings add to the confusion. The nearby Charleston International Airport further increases traffic volume. These design and maintenance issues, combined with heavy commercial truck traffic, make this intersection the most dangerous in the tri-county area.

faqs.question: What should I do if I'm hit by a car as a pedestrian on Rivers Avenue?
faqs.answer: Call 911 immediately and do not move if you suspect spinal injuries. Ask witnesses to stay and provide their contact information. Take photos of the scene if possible. Do not accept any settlement offer from the driver's insurance before consulting an attorney — pedestrian injuries are often severe, and initial offers rarely cover the full cost of treatment. Contact Roden Law for a free consultation.

faqs.question: Which court handles personal injury cases from North Charleston?
faqs.answer: Most personal injury lawsuits arising from North Charleston accidents are filed in Charleston County Circuit Court, located in downtown Charleston. Some areas of North Charleston fall within Berkeley County, in which case the suit may be filed in Berkeley County Circuit Court. Our attorneys practice in both jurisdictions.

Summerville is one of the fastest-growing communities in the Charleston metro area, with approximately 56,000 residents and rapid development that continues to outpace road infrastructure. Known as the 'Flower Town in the Pines,' Summerville sits along the I-26 corridor that connects to downtown Charleston — a stretch of highway identified as one of the most dangerous in South Carolina.

The Carnes Crossroads intersection at US-17A and US-176 has been flagged as a high-collision area, and Dorchester Road's daily commuter congestion produces regular accidents. Roden Law's Charleston attorneys serve Summerville residents and are familiar with the specific road conditions, development patterns, and jurisdictional considerations of Dorchester County cases. Call (843) 790-8999 for a free case review.

faqs.question: My accident happened in Summerville — will my case be in Charleston or Dorchester County?
faqs.answer: Most Summerville accidents fall within Dorchester County jurisdiction, meaning your personal injury lawsuit would be filed in Dorchester County Circuit Court. However, some areas near the Summerville/North Charleston border may fall within Charleston County. Our attorneys practice in both counties and will file your case in the correct jurisdiction.

faqs.question: Is the Carnes Crossroads intersection really that dangerous?
faqs.answer: Yes. The intersection of US-17A and US-176 at Carnes Crossroads recorded 117 collisions in a recent reporting period, making it one of the most dangerous intersections in the tri-county area. The combination of high traffic volumes from rapid residential development, commercial truck traffic, and the intersection's design contribute to frequent serious accidents.

faqs.question: How long does it take to resolve a car accident case from Summerville?
faqs.answer: It depends on the severity of your injuries and the complexity of the case. Straightforward cases with clear liability may settle in 6-12 months. More complex cases involving disputed fault, multiple parties, or catastrophic injuries can take 18 months to 3+ years. South Carolina's 3-year statute of limitations (S.C. Code § 15-3-530) sets the outer deadline for filing.

West Ashley is one of Charleston's largest suburban areas with approximately 75,000 residents spread across a wide geography between the Ashley River and the rural Lowcountry. Savannah Highway (US-17) serves as the primary artery through West Ashley and is one of the most dangerous trucking routes in the Charleston metro — its intersection with Main Road alone accounts for more than 75 truck-related accidents per year.

The Bees Ferry Road corridor has experienced a surge in accidents as new residential developments increase traffic on roads not designed for the volume. The Avondale entertainment district also creates pedestrian safety concerns during evening hours. Roden Law's Charleston office serves all of West Ashley and handles cases involving car accidents, truck accidents, pedestrian injuries, and slip-and-fall incidents throughout the area. Call (843) 790-8999 for a free case review.

faqs.question: Why are there so many truck accidents on Savannah Highway in West Ashley?
faqs.answer: Savannah Highway (US-17) is a primary commercial trucking route that runs directly through West Ashley's residential and commercial areas. The combination of heavy truck traffic, numerous business driveways and intersections, and commuter congestion creates frequent conflict points. Trucks making wide turns at intersections like Savannah Highway and Main Road are a particular hazard.

faqs.question: What if I was injured in a slip-and-fall at a West Ashley business?
faqs.answer: South Carolina premises liability law requires business owners to maintain safe conditions for visitors. If you slipped, tripped, or fell due to a hazardous condition — wet floors, uneven surfaces, poor lighting, or debris — you may have a claim against the property owner. Document the scene with photos, report the incident to the business, and contact Roden Law for a free evaluation of your case.

faqs.question: Is Bon Secours St. Francis the only hospital in West Ashley?
faqs.answer: Bon Secours St. Francis Hospital on Henry Tecklenburg Drive is the primary full-service hospital in West Ashley. For the most severe trauma cases, MUSC Medical Center in downtown Charleston — the region's only Level I Trauma Center — is approximately 15 minutes away. Roper Hospital on Calhoun Street is also accessible from West Ashley.

faqs.question: Can military personnel use Roden Law for off-base accident claims?
faqs.answer: Yes. faqs.question: What is the statute of limitations for a personal injury case in Berkeley County?

Harbor View Road's curves and limited visibility create hazards, especially at night. The single-corridor design of the island means all traffic funnels through Folly Road, amplifying congestion during peak beach season.
hospitals:

James Island is a residential sea island community of approximately 35,000 residents that serves as the gateway to Folly Beach. Nearly all traffic to and from Folly Beach passes through James Island via Folly Road, creating extreme seasonal congestion and year-round accident risks at key intersections.

James Island has the only freestanding emergency room on any of Charleston's sea islands — the HCA Healthcare James Island ER on Folly Road — making it a critical medical resource for the area. Roden Law's Charleston office handles personal injury cases throughout James Island and understands the unique traffic patterns and road conditions that contribute to accidents in this island community.

faqs.question: Is there an emergency room on James Island?
faqs.answer: Yes. HCA Healthcare James Island ER at 945 Folly Road is the only freestanding emergency room on James Island. It is open 24/7 and staffed by board-certified emergency medicine physicians. For severe trauma, MUSC Medical Center in downtown Charleston is approximately 10 minutes away.

faqs.question: Why is Folly Road so dangerous?
faqs.answer: Folly Road is the single primary corridor for all traffic moving between downtown Charleston, James Island, and Folly Beach. This funneling effect creates intense congestion, particularly during summer months and weekends. The road passes through commercial areas with numerous driveways and intersections, each creating conflict points where accidents occur.

faqs.question: I was rear-ended in beach traffic on Folly Road — do I have a case?
faqs.answer: Likely yes. The driver who rear-ended you generally bears the burden of maintaining a safe following distance regardless of traffic conditions. Even in stop-and-go beach traffic, the following driver is expected to remain attentive. Document the scene, get a police report, and contact Roden Law for a free evaluation.

The island's growth has far outpaced its road infrastructure — Maybank Highway remains the primary access corridor, creating a persistent bottleneck that generates accidents daily.

Critically, Johns Island has no full-service emergency room. The nearest full ER is Bon Secours St. Francis in West Ashley, approximately 20 minutes away. MUSC has announced plans for a Sea Islands Medical Pavilion, but until that facility opens, emergency response times on Johns Island remain a serious concern for accident victims. Roden Law's attorneys understand how limited emergency access impacts injury outcomes and will work to ensure your compensation reflects the full scope of your injuries.

faqs.question: Is there an emergency room on Johns Island?
faqs.answer: Not currently. Roper St. Francis has a primary care facility at Freshfields Village, but it is not a full emergency room. The nearest full-service ERs are Bon Secours St. Francis Hospital in West Ashley (approximately 20 minutes) and MUSC Medical Center in downtown Charleston. MUSC has plans for a Sea Islands Medical Pavilion that will bring emergency services closer to Johns Island in the future.

faqs.question: I was in an accident on Bohicket Road — who is responsible for road maintenance?
faqs.answer: Road maintenance responsibility depends on the specific section. Bohicket Road is a state-maintained road under SCDOT jurisdiction. If a road defect — potholes, missing signage, inadequate markings — contributed to your accident, the government entity responsible for maintenance may bear partial liability. Our attorneys investigate all contributing factors including road conditions.

These cases can involve the operator's homeowner's insurance or a specific golf cart liability policy.

faqs.question: I was hit by a drunk driver near Folly Beach — what should I do?
faqs.answer: Call 911 immediately. A DUI accident strengthens your personal injury claim because driving under the influence is negligence per se in South Carolina — meaning the drunk driver's liability is essentially automatic. Do not discuss the accident with the other driver's insurance. Contact Roden Law as soon as possible so we can preserve evidence including police reports and toxicology results.

faqs.question: Is there an emergency room on Folly Beach?
faqs.answer: No. The closest emergency room is HCA Healthcare James Island ER at 945 Folly Road on James Island, approximately 10 minutes from Folly Beach. For severe trauma, MUSC Medical Center is approximately 20 minutes away in downtown Charleston.

content_hook:
Isle of Palms is a barrier island resort community with approximately 4,700 year-round residents and significant seasonal population surges. The IOP Connector bridge is the sole vehicular access point, creating traffic concentration and bridge-related accident risks.

Golf cart collisions, pedestrian injuries at beach crossings, and resort premises liability are common injury scenarios on Isle of Palms. The island has no hospital — the nearest emergency room is East Cooper Medical Center in Mount Pleasant, approximately 15 minutes away.

faqs.question: I was injured in a golf cart accident on Isle of Palms — do I have a case?
faqs.answer: Yes. Golf carts on public roads are subject to South Carolina traffic laws. If another golf cart operator or a motor vehicle driver caused the accident through negligence, you may pursue a personal injury claim. Golf cart accidents on IOP often result in serious injuries because golf carts offer minimal occupant protection.

faqs.question: I was injured at Wild Dunes Resort — who is liable?
faqs.answer: Resort premises liability cases can involve the resort operator, property management company, or specific contractors depending on what caused your injury. Common resort injury scenarios include pool accidents, slip-and-fall incidents, and recreational activity injuries. Contact Roden Law for a free evaluation of your specific situation.

name: Sullivan's Island
h1: Personal Injury Lawyer Serving Sullivan's Island, SC
meta_title: Sullivan's Island Personal Injury Lawyer | Roden Law | Free Consult
meta_description: Injured on Sullivan's Island? Roden Law handles pedestrian, cyclist & vehicle accident cases on this historic barrier island. Free consultation.
roads: Ben Sawyer Boulevard is the primary access route with bridge traffic creating congestion and merge-related accidents. Middle Street is a narrow road shared by vehicles, pedestrians, and cyclists with minimal separation. Island roads are generally narrow with limited shoulders.
hospitals: East Cooper Medical Center — Mount Pleasant, approximately 10 minutes
landmarks: Fort Moultrie National Historical Park, Sullivan's Island Lighthouse, Poe's Gold-Bug Island, Station 22 beach area
service_area: Serving Sullivan's Island, Ben Sawyer Bridge corridor, and connecting to Isle of Palms via Breach Inlet.
content_hook:
Sullivan's Island is a small, historic barrier island of approximately 2,100 residents known for its literary history and relaxed beach atmosphere. The island's narrow roads — particularly Middle Street — are shared by vehicles, pedestrians, cyclists, and golf carts, creating frequent conflict points.

Ben Sawyer Boulevard, the sole vehicular access to the island, channels all traffic across a bridge where accidents and delays are common. Roden Law handles personal injury cases from Sullivan's Island, including pedestrian accidents, cyclist injuries, and golf cart collisions.

faqs.question: I was hit by a car while cycling on Sullivan's Island — what are my rights?
faqs.answer: South Carolina law gives cyclists the same rights and responsibilities as motor vehicle drivers. If a driver struck you due to negligence — failing to maintain safe distance, distracted driving, or failing to yield — you have the right to pursue compensation for your injuries. Sullivan's Island's narrow roads and mixed traffic make these accidents unfortunately common.

name: Downtown Charleston

roads: King Street and Calhoun Street intersection is a high-density pedestrian accident zone due to the mix of tourists, college students, MUSC hospital traffic, and limited parking. The Crosstown Expressway (US-17) bisects the peninsula with high-speed traffic adjacent to residential areas. Meeting Street carries heavy north-south traffic through historic areas with narrow lanes.

MUSC Medical Center (Level I Trauma Center, 700 beds) — 171 Ashley Ave, Charleston, SC 29425
Roper Hospital — 316 Calhoun St, Charleston, SC 29401

content_hook:
Downtown Charleston's historic peninsula is home to approximately 40,000 residents and attracts millions of tourists annually. This concentration of pedestrians, vehicles, horse-drawn carriages, cyclists, and tour groups creates the highest pedestrian accident density in the Charleston metro area.

Roden Law's office is located at 127 King Street in the heart of downtown Charleston. We see firsthand the traffic and pedestrian conflicts that cause injuries on the peninsula — from the Calhoun Street corridor near MUSC to the tourist-heavy Market area. Our attorneys handle pedestrian accidents, vehicle collisions, and premises liability claims throughout the downtown area.

faqs.question: Your office is on King Street — do you handle cases right in downtown Charleston?
faqs.answer: Yes. Our office at 127 King Street, Suite 200 is located in the heart of downtown Charleston. We handle personal injury cases throughout the peninsula and are very familiar with the specific intersections, road conditions, and pedestrian patterns that cause injuries downtown.

faqs.question: I was hit by a carriage tour vehicle downtown — who is liable?
faqs.answer: The carriage tour company and its driver may be liable if negligence caused the accident. Carriage tour operators are required to follow traffic laws and maintain control of their vehicles. These cases can also involve the city's permitting and regulation of carriage tours on public streets. Contact us for a free evaluation.

As the county seat, Berkeley County Courthouse is located in Moncks Corner — the same court where personal injury cases from throughout Berkeley County are filed. Roden Law handles cases in Berkeley County Circuit Court and serves injury victims throughout the Moncks Corner area.

faqs.question: Is there a hospital in Moncks Corner?
faqs.answer: Yes. Roper St. Francis Berkeley Hospital at 730 Stony Landing Road provides emergency and acute care services in Moncks Corner. For more specialized trauma care, Trident Medical Center in North Charleston is approximately 25 minutes south via US-52.

Kiawah Island has no full-service emergency room. The nearest ER is approximately 30 minutes away. This isolation can significantly impact injury outcomes and is an important factor in valuing personal injury claims from the island.

faqs.question: I was injured at the Kiawah Island Resort — who is responsible?
faqs.answer: Liability depends on what caused your injury. The resort, hotel operator, golf course management, pool maintenance company, or property management firm may be liable depending on the circumstances. Premises liability claims at resort properties can be complex and often involve corporate defendants with experienced legal teams. Contact Roden Law for a free evaluation.

faqs.question: I was injured in an equestrian accident on Seabrook Island — can I file a claim?
faqs.answer: South Carolina's equine liability laws provide some protections for equestrian activity operators, but these protections do not extend to negligence. If your injury resulted from negligent supervision, defective equipment, or failure to warn of known hazards, you may have a claim. Contact Roden Law for a free evaluation of your specific circumstances.

The combination of nightlife activity, cycling on mixed-use streets, and proximity to the Rivers Avenue pedestrian fatality corridor makes Park Circle a focus area for personal injury cases including DUI accidents, pedestrian injuries, and cyclist collisions.

faqs.question: I was hit by a car while walking on Rivers Avenue near Park Circle — what should I do?
faqs.answer: Call 911 immediately. Rivers Avenue is one of the most dangerous pedestrian corridors in South Carolina, and pedestrian accidents here tend to be severe due to the road's wide lanes and high traffic speeds. Document the scene, get witness information, and do not give a recorded statement to any insurance company. Contact Roden Law for a free consultation.

faqs.question: I hit a deer on US-17 near Awendaw and was injured — do I have a case?
faqs.answer: While you cannot sue the deer, other parties may be liable depending on the circumstances. If inadequate road signage, missing wildlife crossing warnings, or another driver's negligence contributed to the accident, you may have a claim. Your own uninsured/underinsured motorist coverage may also apply. Contact Roden Law for a free evaluation.

Roden Law handles cases from the Ravenel area, including truck accidents on the Savannah Highway corridor and rural road collisions.

faqs.question: A truck caused my accident on Savannah Highway near Ravenel — can I sue the trucking company?
faqs.answer: Yes. South Carolina law allows you to pursue claims against both the truck driver and the trucking company. The company may be liable under the legal theory of respondeat superior (employer liability) or through its own negligence in hiring, training, or supervising the driver. Our attorneys investigate all potentially liable parties in trucking cases.

The island's agritourism traffic creates a unique DUI risk corridor as visitors travel rural roads after vineyard and brewery visits.

Wadmalaw has no emergency room, and emergency response times to the island can be significant. Roden Law handles cases from Wadmalaw including DUI accidents, rural road collisions, and agricultural road hazards.

faqs.question: I was hit by a drunk driver leaving a Wadmalaw Island vineyard — what are my options?
faqs.answer: You may have claims against both the drunk driver and potentially the establishment that over-served them under South Carolina's dram shop laws. South Carolina allows limited claims against alcohol vendors who serve alcohol to visibly intoxicated persons or minors. Our attorneys investigate all potential sources of liability and compensation.

The island has no hospital, no emergency room, and no urgent care facility. The nearest emergency room is Colleton Medical Center, approximately 30+ minutes away.

This extreme isolation makes accident outcomes on Edisto significantly more serious than comparable accidents closer to Charleston. Premises liability at beach rental properties is also a common issue. Roden Law handles cases from Edisto Island and understands how the island's isolation factors into case valuation and compensation.

faqs.question: I was injured in a beach rental property on Edisto Island — who is liable?
faqs.answer: Liability may fall on the property owner, the rental management company, or both. Common rental property injury scenarios include deck collapses, pool accidents, stairway falls, and failure to maintain safe conditions. South Carolina premises liability law requires property owners and managers to maintain safe conditions for guests. Contact Roden Law for a free evaluation.

faqs.question: Is Edisto Island in Charleston County?
faqs.answer: Edisto Island spans parts of both Charleston County and Colleton County. The specific location of your accident determines jurisdiction. Our attorneys handle cases in both counties and will ensure your case is filed correctly.

McClellanville is a historic shrimping village of approximately 500 residents along US-17 in rural northern Charleston County. The village is one of the most isolated communities in the Charleston metro area, with no local hospital and emergency response times exceeding 40 minutes to the nearest full-service ER.

The US-17 corridor through McClellanville is a high-speed rural highway where serious accidents occur with regularity. The village's commercial fishing industry also creates maritime injury cases under federal admiralty law. Roden Law serves McClellanville residents and handles both motor vehicle and maritime injury claims.

faqs.question: I was injured working on a commercial fishing boat out of McClellanville — does Roden Law handle maritime cases?
faqs.answer: Yes. Maritime injury claims — including Jones Act claims for seamen and Longshore and Harbor Workers' Compensation Act claims — are a practice area of our firm. Maritime law differs significantly from standard personal injury law, with different statutes, filing requirements, and potential damages. Contact us for a free evaluation of your maritime injury case.
